title: Chef Is Just Throwing Random Words

content.problemStatement:
Chef once had a deep epiphany and ended up saying: Given a sequence of positive integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​, if you take each of its 2N2^N2N subsequences and write down the sum of elements of this subsequence, what will the bitwise OR of the written integers be?

Yes, you read it right, but can you solve it?
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated positive integers a1, a2, …, aN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer – the bitwise OR of the sums of all 2^N subsequences of the given array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: OR of elements = 1, total sum = 2, 1|2 = 3. Second test case: OR of elements = 9, total sum = 18, 9|18 = 27.

hidden.label: edge case with powers of two

solution.approach: For any subset its sum is the sum of some of the array elements. The OR of all subset sums certainly contains every bit that appears in any single element, i.e. the OR of all ai. Additionally, when many elements are added together carries can set higher bits. The maximum possible carry effect is observed when all elements are taken, i.e. the total sum S = Σ ai. Every bit set in S can be obtained as a subset sum (by taking the whole array), therefore those bits also belong to the final OR. No other bits can appear because any subset sum is bounded by S, so any bit higher than the highest set bit of S can never be set. Consequently the answer is simply (OR of all ai) | (sum of all ai). The algorithm runs in O(N) per test case, using 64‑bit integers.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(ΣN) over all test cases.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().split()))
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it)
        sum_ = 0
        orv = 0
        for _ in range(N):
            x = next(it)
            sum_ += x
            orv |= x
        out.append(str(sum_ | orv))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
